beats analyze

argo-cd
collapsed clusters
vocabulary-independent structural fingerprinting
2026-05-24 10:09:09
Total Clusters
380
collapsed families
Functions in Clusters
1003
across all clusters
Mean Import Coh.
0.81
mean pairwise Jaccard similarity of function level import
Mean Call Coh.
0.57
mean pairwise Jaccard similarity of function level call targets(fan outs)
High Import · High Call
171
45% — tight domain-local
Low Import · High Call
36
9% — cross-cutting structural
High Import · Low Call
144
38% — domain-cohesive, diverse
Low Import · Low Call
29
8% — probably noise
Metric Glossary
Import Coherence (Coh.)Mean pairwise Jaccard of DirectImports across members. High = every member touches the same package domain (e.g. all use "database/sql"). Measures domain locality.
Call Coherence(Coh.)Mean pairwise Jaccard of CallTargets across members. High = every member invokes the same external functions (e.g. all call RegisterTaskFatal). Measures structural role.
Coherence quadrant guide
High Call Coh.
Low Call Coh.
High Import Coh.
Tight domain-local patternShares both package context and exact call vocabulary. Most actionable.
Domain-cohesive, structurally diverseShared package domain, divergent calls. May need splitting.
Low Import Coh.
Cross-cutting structural patternDifferent domains, same structural role (e.g. cron registration, adapters).
Probably noiseShape coincidence, not convention. Treat with scepticism.
Quadrant cardsEach cluster is placed in one of four quadrants based on whether Import Coh. and Call Coh. are above or below 0.60. The four summary cards at the top show counts per quadrant. Table is sorted by combined score (import + call) / 2 descending by default.
Cyclo P9595th-percentile cyclomatic complexity among cluster members. Cyclo = 1 + decision points (if/for/case). P95 ≥ 10 means at least 5% of functions are complex.
Top ImportsMost frequent DirectImports across cluster members — the shared abstractions that define what this cluster "does".
PackagesGo packages that contribute members to this cluster. Multiple packages = cross-cutting structural pattern. Single package = localised idiom.
Shape HashStable 16-hex identity of the cluster's token sequence. Same hash in two different repos = structurally identical pattern across codebases.
Coherence badge≥ 0.60 tight   0.40–0.59 moderate   < 0.40 loose
Filtered noise Clusters whose shape appears in ≥ 5% of all analysed functions are dropped before this report is generated. These are structural stop-words — patterns so universal (e.g. every model has a fetch-by-ID or a simple getter) that they are technically correct groupings but carry no useful signal. Showing them would dilute the findings that actually reveal conventions and idioms. The function count in the summary cards reflects only the clusters shown here.
Show
Clusters — sorted by combined coherence ↓

Type Label / Shape Size Import Coh. Call Coh. Cyclo P95 Packages Top Imports
